verdict_reasoning: HSPA5/GRP78/BiP is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone with an ER-retention signal (KDEL). However, extensive literature documents its translocation to the cell surface under stress conditions (hypoxia, ER stress, tumorigenesis, viral infection), where it acts as an ecto-chaperone on the outer leaflet of tumor cells and stressed/infected cells. Surface GRP78 is a validated therapeutic target in oncology (antibodies, ADCs, CAR-T approaches targeting cancer-cell surface GRP78). This qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual surface display.
